message:
docs(readme): correct SeriesGroupBy method list (br-l51hq / fd90.135)

fd90.135: line 1184 claimed SeriesGroupBy supports sem/skew/kurtosis
/value_counts, but the impl block at crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:9091
only declares 15 methods:
  sum, mean, count, min, max, rank, rank_with_pct, std, var, median,
  prod, first, last, size, agg

A user calling series.groupby(&other)?.skew()? would hit:
  error[E0599]: no method named 'skew' found for struct 'SeriesGroupBy'

The four phantom methods are likely a confusion with the
DataFrame-level GroupBy aggregation matrix (line 530+) where
skew/kurtosis/sem are listed as agg names available via the string
dispatch. value_counts is a Series method, not a GroupBy method.

Fix: rewrite the SeriesGroupBy method list to exactly match the
impl block, and redirect users wanting the higher-order aggregations
to the existing DataFrame-level GroupBy matrix and Series-direct
methods that DO exist.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Also fixed compilation errors from incomplete SeriesCombineFirst/DataFrameCombineFirst changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-l51hq","title":"fd90.135: SeriesGroupBy claim list adds sem/skew/kurtosis/value_counts that don't exist on it","description":"fd90.74-134 README audit. Line 1184 claims:

  SeriesGroupBy supports: sum, mean, count, min, max, std, var, median,
  first, last, prod, sem, skew, kurtosis, agg (multi-function), and value_counts.

But the SeriesGroupBy impl block at crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:9091 only has 15 methods:
  sum, mean, count, min, max, rank, rank_with_pct, std, var, median, prod,
  first, last, size, agg

Missing methods that README claims exist: sem, skew, kurtosis, value_counts. A user calling series.groupby(&other)?.skew()? would hit:
  error[E0599]: no method named 'skew' found for struct 'SeriesGroupBy'

The DataFrameGroupBy impl (line 26663+) does have skew/kurt/sem etc. (those agg names work via DataFrameGroupBy.agg()), so the prose may have confused the two GroupBy types.

Fix: rewrite the prose to match the actual SeriesGroupBy surface:
  SeriesGroupBy supports: sum, mean, count, min, max, std, var, median,
  first, last, prod, size, rank, rank_with_pct, and agg (multi-function).

The DataFrameGroupBy aggregation table at line 530+ already covers skew/kurtosis/sem/value_counts as DataFrame-level aggs.

Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:34:40.945356427Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:35:59.017575972Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:35:59.014917507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-l6nr","title":"fd90.58: umbrella crate must forward fp-io sql-* features for opt-out support","description":"Self-review of fd90.x feature work surfaced an umbrella crate gap. fp-io has sql-sqlite (default), sql-postgresql, sql-mysql features. The frankenpandas umbrella crate has no features section at all - it pulls fp-io with default features, so users cannot opt out of rusqlite via cargo --no-default-features at the umbrella level. Fix: add features section to umbrella Cargo.toml. default = sql-sqlite. sql-sqlite = fp-io/sql-sqlite. sql-postgresql = fp-io/sql-postgresql. sql-mysql = fp-io/sql-mysql. Also forward default-features = false on the fp-io dep so umbrella users can really opt out. Verify both umbrella build profiles compile + test pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:18:59.795881629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:21:29.274087549Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:21:29.271534990Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","fd90","review","sql"]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-l7o","title":"Add rank metamorphic properties","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T08:25:31.858695556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T08:25:38.886340634Z","closed_at":"2026-04-17T08:25:38.884607413Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-l9pt","title":"fd90.11: SqlReadOptions per-column dtype override","description":"fd90 sub-bead: per-column dtype override on SqlReadOptions matching pd.read_sql(.., dtype=...).

**Scope.** Add `SqlReadOptions::dtype: Option<BTreeMap<String, DType>>`. After row materialization (in `sql_query_to_columns`) but before `dataframe_from_sql_columns`, cast each named column to the requested dtype using `fp_types::cast_scalar_owned`. Columns not in the map are left at the inferred dtype. Map entries for columns not present in the result are silently ignored (matches pandas behavior).

**Reference (pandas 2.2):**
- `pd.read_sql(sql, con, dtype={'amount': 'float64'})` casts the `amount` column.
- Pandas raises ValueError on dtype that can't represent all values; we surface as IoError::Sql.
- pandas IGNORES dtype entries for columns not in the result set; we match.

**Scope guard (this slice):** numeric + Utf8 + Bool + Timedelta64 dtypes only — same set already supported by cast_scalar_owned. Sparse dtype is reserved (Phase 2 of fd90.11 wires it once SparseDType payload semantics solidify).

**Tests (5):** override int→float, override int→utf8, override on missing column (ignored), override on present column with NaN preserved, conflicting dtype-vs-parse_dates rejection ordering (parse_dates wins, dtype skipped for those columns to avoid double-cast errors).

**Blast radius.** 1 field on SqlReadOptions (default None — backwards compat), 1 new private helper apply_sql_dtype_overrides, 1 dispatch site in sql_query_to_columns. Zero call-site touches.

**Severity.** P2.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-25T10:03:37.713067777Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:10:26.821767493Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T10:10:26.819227918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","fd90","parity-gap","review","sql"]}

@@ -1187,7 +1187,7 @@ let prods = by_region.prod()?;
 let multi = by_region.agg(&["sum", "mean", "count"])?;  // Returns DataFrame
 ```
 
-`SeriesGroupBy` supports: `sum`, `mean`, `count`, `min`, `max`, `std`, `var`, `median`, `first`, `last`, `prod`, `sem`, `skew`, `kurtosis`, `agg` (multi-function), and `value_counts`.
+`SeriesGroupBy` supports: `sum`, `mean`, `count`, `min`, `max`, `std`, `var`, `median`, `first`, `last`, `prod`, `size`, `rank`, `rank_with_pct`, and `agg` (multi-function). Higher-order aggregations (`sem`, `skew`, `kurtosis`, `value_counts`) are exposed at the DataFrame-level GroupBy and on `Series` directly — see the GroupBy aggregation matrix above.
 
 ## Sorting
